sentences.target: Mia es estudiante.
sentences.en: Mia is a student.

sentences.target: Vive en un pequeño pueblo.
sentences.en: She lives in a small town.

sentences.target: Cada mañana se despierta temprano.
sentences.en: Every morning she wakes up early.

sentences.target: Bebe té y come pan.
sentences.en: She drinks tea and eats bread.

sentences.target: Luego va a la escuela.
sentences.en: Then she walks to school.

sentences.target: Su escuela está cerca del parque.
sentences.en: Her school is near the park.

sentences.target: Le gusta mucho su profesor.
sentences.en: She likes her teacher very much.

sentences.target: Por la tarde lee libros.
sentences.en: In the afternoon she reads books.

sentences.target: También juega con su perro.
sentences.en: She also plays with her dog.

sentences.target: Por la noche la familia come juntos.
sentences.en: In the evening the family eats together.

sentences.target: Mia está feliz en su pequeña ciudad.
sentences.en: Mia is happy in her small town.

por.note: by the afternoon = no later than.

culturalNote.body: In Spain, small towns (pueblos) are cherished for their slower rhythm, where neighbors greet one another in the plaza and daily life centers on family, school and the local shops.

questions.explanation: «Mia es estudiante» — Mia is a student.
«Vive en un pequeño pueblo» — she lives in a small town.
«Por la tarde lee libros» — in the afternoon she reads books.

sentences.target: Me llamo Tom.
sentences.en: My name is Tom.

sentences.target: Tengo una gran familia.
sentences.en: I have a big family.

sentences.target: Mi madre es doctora.
sentences.en: My mother is a doctor.

sentences.target: Mi padre trabaja en una tienda.
sentences.en: My father works in a shop.

sentences.target: Tengo una hermana y un hermano.
sentences.en: I have one sister and one brother.

sentences.target: Mi hermana es mayor que yo.
sentences.en: My sister is older than me.

sentences.target: Mi hermano tiene sólo cinco años.
sentences.en: My brother is only five years old.

sentences.target: Vivimos en una casa con jardín.
sentences.en: We live in a house with a garden.

sentences.target: El domingo visitamos a mi abuela.
sentences.en: On Sunday we visit my grandmother.

sentences.target: Hace un pastel para todos.
sentences.en: She makes a cake for everyone.

sentences.target: Amo mucho a mi familia.
sentences.en: I love my family very much.

culturalNote.body: In Spain, Sunday is traditionally for family. Many gather for a long midday meal (la comida), and visiting grandparents over the weekend is a cherished custom.

questions.explanation: «Mi madre es doctora» — his mother is a doctor.
«Mi hermano tiene sólo cinco años» — his brother is only five.

questions.stem: Who does the family visit on Sunday?
questions.explanation: «El domingo visitamos a mi abuela» — on Sunday they visit their grandmother.

sentences.target: El sábado por la mañana Anna fue al mercado.
sentences.en: On Saturday morning Anna went to the market.

sentences.target: Quería comprar verduras frescas y frutas.
sentences.en: She wanted to buy fresh vegetables and fruit.

sentences.target: El mercado estaba ocupado y lleno de gente.
sentences.en: The market was busy and full of people.

sentences.target: Primero se detuvo en el depósito de verduras.
sentences.en: First she stopped at the vegetable stall.

sentences.target: "¿Cuánto son los tomates?" preguntó.
sentences.en: "How much are the tomatoes?" she asked.

sentences.target: "Dos euros por kilo," contestó el vendedor.
sentences.en: "Two euros a kilo," the seller answered.

sentences.target: Anna compró tomates, cebollas y lechuga.
sentences.en: Anna bought tomatoes, onions and a lettuce.

sentences.target: Luego caminaba a la panadería.
sentences.en: Next she walked to the bakery.

sentences.target: El olor del pan fresco fue maravilloso.
sentences.en: The smell of fresh bread was wonderful.

sentences.target: Escogió un pan marrón y un poco de queso.
sentences.en: She chose a loaf of brown bread and some cheese.

sentences.target: Antes de irse, compró flores para la mesa.
sentences.en: Before she left, she bought flowers for the table.

sentences.target: Su bolsa era pesada, pero se sentía feliz.
sentences.en: Her bag was heavy, but she felt happy.

sentences.target: Caminaba lentamente en el cálido sol.
sentences.en: She walked home slowly in the warm sun.

sentences.target: Esa noche cocinó una gran cena para sus amigos.
sentences.en: That evening she cooked a big dinner for her friends.

por.note: by the afternoon = no later than.

culturalNote.body: Open-air markets (mercados and mercadillos) remain popular across Spain. Many towns hold a weekly market where locals buy fresh produce, bread and flowers directly from the sellers.

sentences.target: Era viernes, y Sam estaba cansado después de una larga semana.
sentences.en: It was Friday, and Sam was tired after a long week.

sentences.target: Llamó a su amigo Leo por teléfono.
sentences.en: He called his friend Leo on the phone.

sentences.target: "¿Qué haremos este fin de semana?" preguntó.
sentences.en: "What shall we do this weekend?" he asked.

sentences.target: Leo tenía una buena idea.
sentences.en: Leo had a good idea.

sentences.target: "Vamos a las montañas y andemos", dijo.
sentences.en: "Let's go to the mountains and walk," he said.

sentences.target: El sábado por la mañana tomaron el tren temprano.
sentences.en: On Saturday morning they took the early train.

sentences.target: El clima estaba frío pero soleado.
sentences.en: The weather was cold but sunny.

sentences.target: Caminaron durante tres horas por un camino verde.
sentences.en: They walked for three hours along a green path.

sentences.target: En la cima de la colina comieron sus sándwiches.
sentences.en: At the top of the hill they ate their sandwiches.

sentences.target: La vista sobre el valle era hermosa.
sentences.en: The view over the valley was beautiful.

sentences.target: Por la tarde empezó a llover.
sentences.en: In the afternoon it began to rain.

sentences.target: Corrieron a una pequeña cafetería para mantenerse seco.
sentences.en: They ran to a small café to stay dry.

sentences.target: Tomaron café caliente y se reían el día.
sentences.en: They drank hot coffee and laughed about the day.

sentences.target: Fue un fin de semana agotador, pero uno genial.
sentences.en: It was a tiring weekend, but a great one.

por.note: by the afternoon = no later than.

culturalNote.body: Hiking (senderismo) is a much-loved weekend pastime in Spain, from the Pyrenees to the Picos de Europa. Walkers set out with a packed lunch and often end the day at a village bar.

sentences.target: Querido María, finalmente he llegado a Barcelona.
sentences.en: Dear Maria, I have finally arrived in Barcelona.

sentences.target: El viaje fue largo, pero todo fue sin problemas.
sentences.en: The journey was long, but everything went smoothly.

sentences.target: La ciudad es aún más bulliciosa de lo que había imaginado.
sentences.en: The city is even busier than I had imagined.

sentences.target: En mi primer día, simplemente caminé alrededor del centro.
sentences.en: On my first day, I simply walked around the center.

sentences.target: Crucé el río en un hermoso puente viejo.
sentences.en: I crossed the river on a beautiful old bridge.

sentences.target: Aunque llovía, no me importaba nada.
sentences.en: Although it was raining, I did not mind at all.

sentences.target: Ayer visité un famoso museo cerca de la estación.
sentences.en: Yesterday I visited a famous museum near the station.

sentences.target: La entrada era gratuita, lo que me sorprendió.
sentences.en: The entrance was free, which surprised me.

sentences.target: Pasé toda la tarde mirando pinturas.
sentences.en: I spent the whole afternoon looking at paintings.

sentences.target: Después encontré una cafetería tranquila y escribí en mi cuaderno.
sentences.en: Afterwards, I found a quiet café and wrote in my notebook.

sentences.target: La gente aquí ha sido amable y servicial.
sentences.en: The people here have been friendly and helpful.

sentences.target: Cuando pedí direcciones, un extraño me llevó a la esquina.
sentences.en: When I asked for directions, a stranger walked me to the corner.

sentences.target: Mañana planeo tomar un tren a la costa.
sentences.en: Tomorrow I plan to take a train to the coast.

sentences.target: He oído que los acantilados son espectaculares.
sentences.en: I have heard that the cliffs there are spectacular.

sentences.target: Si tienes tiempo el próximo verano, deberías venir.
sentences.en: If you have time next summer, you really should come.

sentences.target: Me encantaría mostrarte todos mis lugares favoritos.
sentences.en: I would love to show you all my favorite places.

sentences.target: Cuídate y escríbete pronto.
sentences.en: Take care of yourself, and write back soon.

sentences.target: Con amor, Daniel.
sentences.en: With love, Daniel.

culturalNote.body: Spain has many world-class museums, and several offer free entry at certain times — including Madrid's Prado on weekday evenings and many of Barcelona's museums on the first Sunday of the month.

sentences.target: Sofía había estado buscando un trabajo durante varios meses.
sentences.en: Sofia had been looking for a job for several months.

sentences.target: Por fin, una pequeña empresa le ofreció una posición.
sentences.en: At last, a small company offered her a position.

sentences.target: En su primera mañana, se sintió excitada y nerviosa.
sentences.en: On her first morning, she felt both excited and nervous.

sentences.target: Llegó diez minutos antes para dar una buena impresión.
sentences.en: She arrived ten minutes early to make a good impression.

sentences.target: Una amiga la conoció en la entrada.
sentences.en: A friendly colleague met her at the entrance.

sentences.target: La mostró por la oficina y presentó al equipo.
sentences.en: He showed her around the office and introduced the team.

sentences.target: Todos parecían acogedores, lo que la ayudó a relajarse.
sentences.en: Everyone seemed welcoming, which helped her to relax.

sentences.target: Su gerente explicó las principales tareas de la semana.
sentences.en: Her manager explained the main tasks for the week.

sentences.target: Al principio, el nuevo software era difícil de entender.
sentences.en: At first, the new software was difficult to understand.

sentences.target: Sin embargo, hizo preguntas y aprendió lentamente el sistema.
sentences.en: However, she asked questions and slowly learned the system.

sentences.target: Durante el almuerzo, habló con sus colegas en la cocina.
sentences.en: During lunch, she chatted with her colleagues in the kitchen.

sentences.target: Le dieron consejos útiles sobre la compañía.
sentences.en: They gave her useful advice about the company.

sentences.target: Por la tarde, ya se sentía más segura.
sentences.en: By the afternoon, she already felt more confident.

sentences.target: Terminó su primer informe justo antes de las cinco.
sentences.en: She finished her first report just before five o'clock.

sentences.target: En el camino a casa, se sonrió.
sentences.en: On the way home, she smiled to herself.

sentences.target: El día había ido mucho mejor de lo que esperaba.
sentences.en: The day had gone far better than she had expected.

sentences.target: Sabía que habría desafíos por delante.
sentences.en: She knew there would be challenges ahead.

sentences.target: Pero por ahora, estaba orgullosa de cómo había comenzado.
sentences.en: But for now, she was proud of how she had begun.

por.note: by the afternoon = no later than.

culturalNote.body: In many Spanish workplaces, a new employee's first day includes a tour, introductions to the team and often a relaxed coffee together. Colleagues tend to be warm and approachable.
